description: ol-style-block-indentation (issue #169): an `… end` block's second statement (print 2, column 4) is indented one column deeper than its sibling (print 1, column 3), disagreeing with the block's own majority/baseline column. This is a long-form block (no bracket delimiter), so it never triggers ol-style-prefer-block, and the two statements are on separate lines, so it never triggers ol-style-one-command-per-line — an isolated identity check for this lint alone.
